title: Executive Order 9622—Revocation of Executive Order 9103 of March 18, 1942, Providing Uniform Control Over the Publication and Use of Federal Statistical Information Which Would Give Aid and Comfort to the Enemy

flags.justification: A pure revocation; no new decision-making authority is created.
No ongoing program is created by a revocation.
Single purpose: revoking the named prior order.
A revocation removes a compliance regime rather than creating one.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A one-time revocation grants no ongoing authority requiring reauthorization.
Revoking a wartime statistical-secrecy control restores informational openness rather than removing an oversight or check mechanism.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A straightforward postwar policy reversal with no elaborated broader effect in the text.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/10 = 0.0%. Part of the same postwar wind-down wave as EO 9607/9608/9621, all signed within three weeks of each other.
